name: Riaza - Ayllón Red Villages Loop
name_original: Ayllón desde Riaza — circular por Madriguera
description: This moderate 34.4-mile road cycling route from Riaza explores the scenic Sierra de Ayllón, passing through medieval Ayllón and Madriguera.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Riaza offer diverse and scenic landscapes, blending mountainous terrain with river gorges and historical areas. The region, situated in the Segovia province of Castilla y León, Spain, features varied topography including the Sierra de Ayllón mountain range and the dramatic limestone cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. Local roads are noted for minimal traffic, providing an optimal environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Riaza The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de la Quesera Climb from Riaza, a 16.0 miles (25.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a well-known climb with a good road surface. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Riaza - Ayllón Red Villages Loop, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.4 km) path. This route explores picturesque medieval towns and offers views of the Sierra de Ayllón. Local road cyclists also love the Castillejo de Mesleón from Riaza — circular by the Río Serrano, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through river valleys, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Road cycling around Riaza is defined by mountainous terrain, river gorges, and traditional villages, with routes offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Riaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 650 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Riaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Riaza?
A: Road cycling around Riaza offers a diverse landscape, from the challenging mountainous terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Ayllón&lt;/strong&gt;, including the well-known Puerto de la Quesera, to scenic river gorges and rolling hills. You'll encounter dramatic limestone cliffs in the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park and picturesque valleys, often on roads with minimal traffic.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Riaza?
A: Yes, Riaza offers options for all skill levels. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are also easier paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352479324/castillejo-de-mesleon-from-riaza-circular-by-the-rio-serrano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo de Mesleón from Riaza — circular by the Río Serrano&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 15.8-mile route leading through river valleys, perfect for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Riaza?
A: Absolutely. Riaza is known for its demanding climbs, particularly in the Sierra de Ayllón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352451965/puerto-de-la-quesera-climb-from-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera Climb from Riaza&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, a well-known ascent that has even been featured in the Vuelta a España. There are 13 difficult routes available, offering significant elevation gains for seasoned cyclists.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural attractions can I discover while road cycling in Riaza?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the dramatic gorges and canyons of the &lt;strong&gt;Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, home to one of Europe's largest colonies of griffon vultures. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Ayllón&lt;/strong&gt; provides stunning mountain vistas. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera&lt;/a&gt;, a notable mountain pass offering expansive views.

Q: Are there any interesting historical towns or landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through charming traditional villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352428377/riaza-ayllon-red-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaza - Ayllón Red Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores picturesque medieval towns like Ayllón and Madriguera. Riaza itself boasts an old town with a distinctive elliptical Plaza Mayor and the impressive Renaissance-style Church of Nuestra Señora del Manto.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Riaza?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Riaza, catering to various difficulty levels. This includes 1 easy route, 13 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for every type of rider.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Riaza?
A: The road cycling routes in Riaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse and scenic landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the minimal traffic on local roads, which enhances the overall riding experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer challenges, Riaza offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068258032/puerto-de-la-quesera-y-los-pueblos-negros-de-la-sierra-de-ayllon-circular-por-el-castillo-de-galve-de-sorbe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera y los pueblos negros de la Sierra de Ayllón — circular por el Castillo de Galve de Sorbe&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 94 miles (152 km) with significant elevation gain, perfect for endurance training.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Riaza?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Riaza are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352428377/riaza-ayllon-red-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaza - Ayllón Red Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352479324/castillejo-de-mesleon-from-riaza-circular-by-the-rio-serrano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo de Mesleón from Riaza — circular by the Río Serrano&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient round trips.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Riaza?
A: While not explicitly stated, the region's mountainous terrain suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or ice in winter at higher elevations. The good road surfaces and light traffic are consistent year-round benefits.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes I can cycle to in the Riaza area?
A: Yes, the region is home to several notable mountain passes that are popular with cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly famous and challenging climb. Other passes in the vicinity include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Aventadero&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2692440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Puerto de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied ascents and descents.
tips: As you approach the old city from the west and cross the Aguisejo River, the impressive medieval gate in the city wall admires you in all its splendor.

The best known of the red towns on the Route of the Red and Black Towns of Segovia, for being the most representative of the so-called "red architecture." The houses have their color thanks to the use of the reddish elements of the iron stone and the clay soil of this area.
In the old middle of the town of Riaza is this beautiful round square that is not paved but made of dirt. Beautiful square with the church as a central part.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ayllón from Riaza — circular via Madriguera route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as a moderate road ride. It covers approximately 55.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of 610 meters, requiring good fitness. While mostly well-paved, some sections might include cobblestones or other surfaces that add to the challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly asphalt, covering about 54.2 kilometers. You will also encounter smaller sections of paved track, some unknown surfaces, and approximately 139 meters of cobblestones, particularly in the historic towns. Overall, it features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ayllón from Riaza — circular via Madriguera route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can enjoy a mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Highlights include the stunning landscapes with rugged mountains and endless fields, the pine forests, and the jagged peaks of the Sierra de Ayllón in the background. You'll pass through the beautiful medieval town of Ayllón, known for its cobblestone streets and ancient stone arches, and the unique village of Madriguera, part of the distinctive 'red and black villages' of the region.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car when starting this route in Riaza?
faqs.answer: You can find parking options in Riaza, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Look for parking in the town center or designated visitor parking areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring, summer, and autumn. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions for road cycling, allowing you to fully enjoy the stunning landscapes and historic towns without the challenges of winter weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Ayllón from Riaza — circular via Madriguera route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Is the Ayllón from Riaza — circular via Madriguera route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its length and elevation gain. While it offers beautiful scenery, it might be challenging for beginners or young children. Families looking for easier options might consider shorter, flatter routes in the region.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this route primarily follows public roads and paved tracks, dogs are generally allowed, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you for the duration of the ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route in Ayllón or Riaza?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Riaza and Ayllón are towns with amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in both locations, making them convenient stops for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta del Sureste, the Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, and the Camino de Estebanvela, among others. These intersections are generally short segments of the overall route.

faqs.question: What makes the architecture in Madriguera unique?
faqs.answer: Madriguera is one of the distinctive 'red and black villages' in the region. Its unique architecture uses local materials like black slate and red clay, giving the houses their characteristic coloring and a timeless appearance, making it a notable cultural highlight on the route.
